name: Turó de l'Home y Les Agudes — circular desde Plana del Coll
region_intro: Hiking around Fogars De Montclús offers access to diverse landscapes within the Montseny Natural Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, and the nearby Parc Natural del Corredor-Montnegre. The region features significant elevation changes, from lower streams to peaks like Turó de l'Home at 1,707 meters. Hikers encounter varied ecosystems, including lush oak, chestnut, and beech forests, as well as water bodies such as the Santa Fe Reservoir. This area provides a range of trails suitable for different skill levels, characterized by its natural features and varied terrain. Best hiking trails around Fogars De Montclús The most popular hiking route is Empedrat de Morou and Turó de Morou Trail, a 3.8 miles (6.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 54 minutes to complete. This easy path offers forest sections and panoramic views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Santa Fe Reservoir Loop, an easy 1.6 miles (2.6 km) path. This circular route features views of the reservoir and a waterfall. Local hikers also love the Turó de l'Home y Les Agudes — circular desde Plana del Coll, a 8.8 miles (14.1 km) trail leading through mountain terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 45 minutes. Hiking around Fogars De Montclús is defined by diverse forests, significant mountain peaks, and scenic reservoirs. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to challenging ascents. The routes in Fogars De Montclús are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 3500 hikers have used komoot to explore Fogars De Montclús's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Fogars De Montclús?
A: There are over 45 hiking trails around Fogars De Montclús, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix, with 12 easy routes, 27 moderate trails, and 7 challenging options for experienced hikers.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Fogars De Montclús?
A: The trails in Fogars De Montclús are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 400 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse forests, scenic reservoirs, and the well-maintained paths within the Montseny Natural Park.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Fogars De Montclús?
A: Yes, Fogars De Montclús offers several easy trails perfect for beginners and families. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1358581950/santa-fe-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Fe Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 1.6-mile path featuring reservoir views and a waterfall. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1358572974/empedrat-de-morou-and-turo-de-morou-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Empedrat de Morou and Turó de Morou Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.8 miles long and offers pleasant forest sections.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Fogars De Montclús?
A: For experienced hikers, Fogars De Montclús provides several challenging routes, particularly those leading to the prominent peaks of Montseny. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1358537094/turo-de-l-home-y-les-agudes-circular-desde-plana-del-coll" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turó de l'Home y Les Agudes — circular desde Plana del Coll&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 8.8-mile trail that takes you through significant mountain terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 45 minutes. These routes offer substantial elevation changes and panoramic views.

Q: Can I find trails with waterfalls or wild swimming spots in Fogars De Montclús?
A: Yes, the region is home to beautiful waterfalls and natural pools, especially along the Riera de Gualba, which are ideal for refreshing dips in warmer months. You can explore highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/879070" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorg Negre Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3303255" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molí de Baix Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1358581950/santa-fe-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Fe Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; also features a waterfall along its path.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Fogars De Montclús?
A: Yes, many trails in Fogars De Montclús are circular, offering varied scenery without retracing your steps. Popular circular options include the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1358581950/santa-fe-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Fe Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1358537094/turo-de-l-home-y-les-agudes-circular-desde-plana-del-coll" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turó de l'Home y Les Agudes — circular desde Plana del Coll&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1358572974/empedrat-de-morou-and-turo-de-morou-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Empedrat de Morou and Turó de Morou Trail&lt;/a&gt; is also a circular route.

Q: What kind of natural features and landscapes can I expect to see while hiking?
A: Hiking in Fogars De Montclús, located within the Montseny Natural Park, offers incredibly diverse landscapes. You'll encounter lush forests of oak, chestnut, and beech trees, significant mountain peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1687529" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turó de l'Home&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/988421" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Agudes&lt;/a&gt;, and scenic water bodies such as the Santa Fe Reservoir. The area is a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, known for its rich biodiversity and varied ecosystems.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the hiking trails?
A: Beyond natural beauty, Fogars De Montclús is dotted with historical and cultural sites. You can find ancient farmhouses, some preserving traditional Montseny characteristics, and historical sites like the Romanesque Church of San Martí d'Ogassa. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/132844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Pedra Gentil&lt;/a&gt; is another notable historical monument in the area.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Fogars De Montclús?
A: The Montseny Natural Park is beautiful year-round, but spring and autumn are particularly popular for hiking due to milder temperatures and vibrant foliage. Spring brings blooming wildflowers, while autumn offers stunning colors in the deciduous forests. Summer can be warm, but higher elevation trails provide cooler conditions. Winter hiking is also possible, especially at lower altitudes, though some higher routes may require appropriate gear for snow or ice.

Q: Are there any trails that offer panoramic viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. The Montseny massif is renowned for its breathtaking panoramic views. Trails leading to peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1687529" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turó de l'Home&lt;/a&gt; (1,706m), &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/988421" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Agudes&lt;/a&gt; (1,703m), and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/226426" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matagalls&lt;/a&gt; (1,697m) offer expansive vistas that can stretch from the Pyrenees to the Costa Brava on clear days. The Pla de la Calma area also provides exceptional viewpoints over the Tordera valley.

Q: Is there public transport or parking available near the trailheads?
A: While specific public transport options to all trailheads might be limited, the Montseny Natural Park has visitor centers and some areas, like the Santa Fe car park, serve as common starting points for popular routes. It's advisable to check local transport schedules or plan for car access, as parking is generally available at key entry points to the park and popular trailheads.
tips:
If you need inner peace, if you want to touch the maximum of your emotions and bare your soul, you have to climb the Turó de l'Home, because
its spectacular views will fill your senses with an indescribable magic.

Located at an altitude of 1,667 meters on the Puig Sesolles near the Turó de l'Home, the transmission detachment was built in 1974, with capacity for about 70 soldiers, for which it had turret and parabolic antennas. It also had a soldier's home building with a library and a heliport.

In 2010 the Servei Meteorològic de Catalunya installed new automatic measurement equipment. The building has a perimeter protection fence.
Section connecting the Turó de l'Home to Les Agudes. Not too difficult and without a great difference in height, the last piece before Les Agudes is very steep and rocky.

Les Agudes is a 1706 meter high mountain that is part of the Montseny massif. It is located in the municipalities of Arbucias, Fogás de Monclús and Montseny, between the regions of Vallés Oriental and La Selva in the provinces of Barcelona and Gerona, in the autonomous community of Catalonia, Spain.
